items.title: Solana Surpasses Ethereum in RWA Holders
items.author: @solana

ctPulse.thought: BREAKING: Solana flipped Ethereum in total RWA holders for the first time, according to @RWA_xyz 🤯 https://t.co/cOQ4VezJn2
Agents got smarter with the identity and payment rails to back it up. Consumer products shipped across trading terminals to privacy tools. Institutions continued expanding onchain with the capital to

"To run stablecoins, you need stable rails. You need a network that is secure, efficient, fast, and cheap. You can't find anyone better than Solana." - Shawn Chan, CEO of @SGB_app https://t.co/DQD3FEv
RT @solana: BREAKING: Solana flipped Ethereum in total RWA holders for the first time, according to @RWA_xyz 🤯 https://t.co/cOQ4VezJn2
BREAKING: @xStocksFi is bringing Nasdaq's tokenized equity markets to Solana DeFi

whoToFollow.reason: Tracks protocol growth and RWA adoption trends

content.signal: Solana now leads Ethereum in total real-world asset (RWA) holders, according to RWA_xyz.
content.story:
Solana has overtaken Ethereum in the number of real-world asset (RWA) holders for the first time, as reported by RWA_xyz. This marks a significant milestone in the ongoing competition between major blockchains for dominance in the tokenization and onchain asset space. The shift reflects changing user preferences and growing institutional activity on Solana’s network.

### What's Happening

Solana’s ecosystem has seen a surge in RWA adoption, with more users holding tokenized assets like equities and stablecoins directly onchain. Institutional players are expanding their presence, supported by advancements in identity and payment infrastructure. Recent announcements highlight that Nasdaq’s tokenized equity markets are being brought to Solana DeFi, and protocols such as Kamino have become the largest by total value locked (TVL) due in part to their RWA focus.

Solana’s technical strengths—speed, low fees, and efficient settlement—are being cited by ecosystem leaders as critical for stablecoin and RWA operations. Consumer-facing products, privacy tools, and trading terminals are shipping at a rapid pace, further driving user engagement and capital inflows.

### Why It Matters

Flipping Ethereum in RWA holders signals a shift in where users and institutions are choosing to interact with tokenized assets. Solana’s ability to attract both retail and institutional capital suggests it is becoming a preferred platform for scalable, real-world financial applications. This momentum is reinforced by high trading volumes, growing developer activity, and new products launching across DeFi, NFTs, and hardware devices.

The network’s revenue generation has also outpaced Ethereum in recent months, indicating robust ecosystem activity. With stable infrastructure and a focus on user experience, Solana is positioning itself as a foundational layer for the next wave of onchain finance.

### The Risk or Debate

Rapid growth in RWA adoption brings scrutiny around security, compliance, and network reliability. As more capital and institutional players move onchain, the risk of technical failures or regulatory challenges increases. The sustainability of high yields and the safety of new protocols remain open questions, especially as the ecosystem continues to scale.

There is also debate over whether Solana’s infrastructure can maintain its performance under heavier loads and more complex use cases. The competition with Ethereum and other chains is ongoing, and shifts in user or developer sentiment could quickly change the landscape.

### What To Watch

Upcoming developments include further institutional integrations, new stablecoin and RWA product launches, and the results of ongoing hackathons aimed at expanding Solana’s application stack. Monitoring the security and uptime of the network will be key as adoption grows. The evolution of regulatory frameworks and how projects respond to compliance requirements will also shape the future trajectory of RWAs on Solana.
takeaways: Solana has surpassed Ethereum in total RWA holders for the first time.
Institutional and retail adoption of tokenized assets is accelerating on Solana.
Network growth brings both new opportunities and increased scrutiny around security and compliance.

whoToFollow.reason: Primary source for platform updates and new features
Key NFT partner in the SBT collaboration

content.signal: Jupiter Exchange and Pudgy Penguins have launched a mobile SBT token, signaling deeper integration and expanding user engagement in the Solana ecosystem.
content.story:
Jupiter Exchange and Pudgy Penguins have rolled out a new mobile Soulbound Token (SBT), available for a limited time to users who complete a swap in the Jupiter wallet before March 20. This move adds another layer to Jupiter’s growing suite of user incentives and ecosystem integrations, following a series of airdrops, presale opportunities, and new product launches.

### What's Happening

The Pudgy x Jupiter Mobile SBT is now live, offering users a new way to participate in both communities. Claiming the SBT requires a simple wallet swap, making the process accessible and encouraging wallet activity. This launch comes alongside other recent Jupiter updates, including the release of the Jupiter Card—a Visa-powered in-wallet card for daily spending—and the introduction of Comet Trading, a Telegram-based trading bot.

Jupiter’s stablecoin, $JupUSD, has also seen rapid growth, reaching $67 million in total supply within a few months. The stablecoin is fully backed, with 82% in USDtb and 18% in USDC, reflecting a focus on transparency and stability. Meanwhile, new yield opportunities are emerging through integrations with platforms like Hubra, which offers access to both Kamino and Jupiter Lend venues in a single vault.

### Why It Matters

The SBT launch signals Jupiter’s intent to deepen user engagement and strengthen ties with influential NFT brands like Pudgy Penguins. By making participation easy and rewarding, Jupiter is positioning itself as a central hub for Solana-native activity, from trading to payments to NFT-based rewards. The rapid adoption of $JupUSD and the rollout of the Jupiter Card suggest that the platform is moving beyond swaps, aiming to become a comprehensive financial layer within Solana.

These developments are also broadening the appeal of Solana’s ecosystem. By integrating NFTs, stablecoins, and DeFi products, Jupiter is lowering the barrier for new users and offering existing participants more ways to interact and benefit. The ongoing series of airdrops, presale access, and new token launches keeps the community engaged and incentivized.

### The Risk or Debate

The pace of new product launches and reward programs raises questions about sustainability and user fatigue. While SBTs and airdrops can drive engagement, there is a risk that users may become desensitized if rewards lose perceived value. The reliance on stablecoins, even with full backing, also introduces exposure to underlying asset risks and potential regulatory scrutiny.

Integrations with multiple platforms and the rapid scaling of new features may introduce technical or security risks. The complexity of managing cross-platform incentives and products could lead to confusion or operational challenges for less experienced users.

### What To Watch

The limited-time SBT claim window closes on March 20, so user participation metrics will reveal the level of engagement. Adoption and usage of the Jupiter Card and Comet Trading bot will indicate whether Jupiter can successfully expand beyond swaps into payments and social trading. The growth trajectory of $JupUSD and the performance of new yield products on platforms like Hubra will be key indicators of ecosystem health and user trust.

Continued collaboration with NFT brands and further airdrop or presale announcements may signal Jupiter’s next moves in community building and product expansion.
takeaways: Jupiter and Pudgy Penguins launched a mobile SBT, claimable via wallet swap until March 20.
Jupiter is expanding its ecosystem with a stablecoin, in-wallet Visa card, and trading bot.
Rapid product launches and reward programs raise sustainability and user engagement questions.

whoToFollow.reason: Official source for network updates and ecosystem growth.
Provides analytics on real-world asset trends and on-chain data.

content.signal: Solana processed $650B in stablecoin volume in February, more than any other network and over twice its previous record.
content.story:
Solana just posted a new milestone: $650 billion in stablecoin volume moved across its network in February. This figure not only tops all other blockchains for the month, but also more than doubles Solana’s previous record. The surge comes as Solana’s infrastructure for payments and identity continues to mature, and as more consumer-facing products go live.

### What's Happening
Stablecoins—crypto tokens pegged to the value of fiat currencies—are seeing unprecedented activity on Solana. The network’s payment rails and identity features have become more robust, supporting everything from trading terminals to private consumer apps. Meanwhile, the market cap for real-world assets (RWAs) on Solana has grown roughly 10x over the past year, reflecting broader adoption of tokenized funds, stocks, and commodities.

This momentum is being noticed by builders and users alike. The combination of high throughput, low fees, and improved developer tooling is making Solana a preferred platform for stablecoin transactions and new financial products. The network’s ability to handle large volumes efficiently is a key factor in this growth.

### Why It Matters
Stablecoin volume is a strong proxy for real economic activity on-chain. Solana’s outsized numbers suggest it is becoming a central hub for payments, trading, and asset transfers. This is significant because it signals growing trust in Solana’s infrastructure and its ability to support both retail and institutional use cases.

The rapid expansion of RWAs on Solana points to a broader trend: traditional financial assets are being brought on-chain at scale. This could open the door to new products and services that blend crypto-native speed with real-world value. For the Solana ecosystem, these developments could attract more developers, users, and capital.

### The Risk or Debate
With rapid growth comes scrutiny. High stablecoin volumes can attract regulatory attention, especially as real-world assets are tokenized and moved on-chain. There are also technical risks: network congestion, smart contract bugs, or exploits could undermine confidence if not managed carefully. Finally, questions remain about how sustainable this pace of growth is, and whether it’s driven by organic demand or short-term incentives.

### What To Watch
Keep an eye on how Solana’s payment and identity rails evolve, especially as more consumer products launch. Watch for further growth in tokenized real-world assets and how regulators respond to these developments. Also, monitor the network’s stability and performance as volumes continue to rise. The next few months will reveal whether Solana can maintain its lead in stablecoin and RWA activity without hitting technical or regulatory roadblocks.
takeaways: Solana processed $650B in stablecoin volume in February, leading all blockchains.
Real-world asset market cap on Solana has grown nearly 10x in a year.
Growth in payments and identity rails is enabling new consumer and trading products.
